body_txt: Usually as a person gets older, they begin to lose some of their capabilities, especially those that affect physical fitness. Older folks can and do still enjoy light exercise, but their adventuring days are over. Usually. Not this guy. This character is up there in age (and their body shows it) but don't let their appearance fool you, they can and will sign on for the adventure. Great-Grandpa wants to keep his strength up as much as he can, so why not volunteer to carry loads of firewood for the camp every night? Aunt Millie just celebrated her 87th birthday, but she'd rather run a half-marathon than sit and play solitaire. To them, age is just a number, and it's one they're ignoring. Note this trope is for examples where a character is depicted as more physically capable than their age would normally lead others to believe. The 700-year-old vampire lord who looks 40 and wipes the floor with the protagonist is not an example. The 90-year-old grandma fighting and keeping up with her 25-year-old granddaughter is. See also Old Superhero and Old Master , an exaggerated form of this trope, often applying to older mentor-type characters, and Older Sidekick when the spry old guy is the hero's number two. If they're shown as physically capable when fighting, it can overlap with When Elders Attack and/or Never Mess with Granny as well. May also overlap with Evil Old Folks . Contrast Feeling Their Age .
